pages.18:
Technical Competence 
GTBLLP is working in close alignment with the vision of Government of India towards creating a vibrant Bharat. The 
firm has been providing services to various central government ministries, state government authorities and 
Multilateral & Bilateral agencies such as World Bank and ADB to support critical development initiatives in the 
country. We bring on board a varied set of perspectives gained through our multisectoral experience, strong technical 
know-how, project management skills which is complemented by a qualified pool of internal and external experts 
having exposure of working on large scale assignments and flagship programs across the country.  
Experience in Education and Skill Development 
GTBLLP has rich experience around enhancing quality of education, vocationalisation of education, Technical and 
Vocational Education and Training (TVET), livelihood generation through skill development, entrepreneurship, and 
innovation, gained through program management support and technical support to diverse set of clients.  
GTBLLP also served as State Technical Support Agency (STSA) for DDUGKY under Maharashtra State Rural 
Livelihoods Mission (MSRLM)- DDUGKY State Mission Management Unit, Maharashtra State Rural Livelihoods 
Mission, that involved IEC, Mobilization, Counselling, Migration Support Services and ensuring Capacity 
Building of training partners.  
GTBLLP has been providing program management support to Government of India’s flagship programmes like Skills 
Acquisition and Knowledge Awareness for Livelihood Promotion (SANKALP), Scheme for Capacity Building in 
Textile Sector (SAMARTH), Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDUGKY), State Rural 
Livelihood Mission (SRLM), etc. that have together facilitated training of about 1.5 Mn and placement of about 1.1 
Mn candidates across sectors like Retail, Hospitality, Logistics, Telecom, IT/ITeS, Textile, Automotive, Electronics, 
Healthcare, Aviation etc. Through these programs, we have enabled placement linked training to youth from 
diverse background, enhancement, and creation of vocational training infrastructure, support services for 
incubation, development, and accreditation of new job roles in line with National Skills Qualification Framework 
(NSQF), policy framing and formulating strategies for implementation of skill development trainings, awareness 
generation, self-employment and micro-enterprise development, recognition of prior learning, livelihood 
generation, etc. The firm has also facilitated these programs in creating robust technology-based management 
and monitoring systems along with facilitating support activities like procurement of services and civil works, 
financial management, capacity building of stakeholders and knowledge sharing.  
The firm provided consultancy services to State project Implementation Unit, Directorate of Training, Uttarakhand 
for designing a strategic results-oriented partnership framework with industry and private sector bodies to 
help increase the number of MOUs with industries as well as the coverage of trades benefiting from industry 
engagement, setup Industry linkage centres and capacity building across the priority Industrial Training 
Institutes (ITIs).  
GTBL has been providing program management support to Government of India’s flagship programmes like, 
SAMARTH, DDUGKY, etc. that have together facilitated training of about 1.5 Mn and placement of about 1.1 Mn 
candidates across sectors like Retail, Hospitality, Logistics, Telecom, IT/ITeS, Textile, Automotive, Electronics, 
Healthcare, Aviation etc. Through these programs, we have enabled placement linked training to youth from 
diverse background, enhancement, and creation of vocational training infrastructure, support services for 
incubation, development, and accreditation of new job roles in line with NSQF, policy framing and formulating 
strategies for implementation of skill development trainings, awareness generation, self-employment and micro-
enterprise development, recognition of prior learning, livelihood generation, etc. The firm has also facilitated 
these programs in creating robust technology-based management and monitoring systems along with 
facilitating support activities like procurement of services and civil works, financial management, capacity 
building of stakeholders and knowledge sharing.

Description of Approach, Methodology 
Our Understanding of the Assignment 
Context and Background 
The Ministry of Rural Development (MoRD) is implementing Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana 
(DDU-GKY) with a vision to “transform rural poor youth into an economically independent and globally relevant 
workforce”. DDU-GKY is a part of the National Rural Livelihood Mission (NRLM), tasked with the dual objectives of 
adding diversity to the incomes of rural poor families and cater to the career aspirations of rural youth. 
Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY) aims to provide placement to skilled human 
resource. DDU-GKY occupies a unique position amongst other skill training programmes, due to its focus on the 
rural poor youth and its emphasis on sustainable employment through the prominence and incentives for Higher 
placements. 
The trainings across the various skill development programs have not kept pace with the changing requirements of 
the industry and in particular desired need of each and product differentiated employer. The courses being run 
currently are as per general norms of NSQF. The employment potential of each of the course not ascertained and 
Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs) adopt some of them based on their training ability/feasibility. After training 
the trainees are placed with a different job role, which puts a question mark on the job roles for which the trainee 
was trained on. Thus, there is currently a peculiar concern of “un-employment of trained candidates” and “lack of 
skilled human resource as perceived by Industry”. To train trainees in such a way that they are industry ready from 
day one, it is inevitable to develop/ utilize a model in which training too is provided by the skilled labour absorbing 
enterprise, which provides the Captive employment to the skilled manpower. 
Since every effort is made to skill the candidates as per the industry standards, the absorption rate of candidates 
trained by PIA is still low in the market, as each industry/employment has different level of needs and that too 
changes over a period of time. The skilling for performing a particular job requires trainees to accustom with relevant 
job operations being conducted at actual locations. Also, it becomes quite challenging for the industry, which 
requires large number of human resources to set up separate skilling infrastructure other than industry as per 
standards of govt. schemes, when they already have the readily available infrastructure for providing skilling as per 
their own industry standards. On the other hand, If Industry is encouraged in adopting Captive employer model, it 
gives confirm employment to the skilled candidates which in-turn also provide them real work life environment to 
learn skills on the job.  
In order to encourage the participation of Industry and tailor made the candidates as per the industry requirement, 
DDU-GKY is facilitating Captive Employment model of skilling. The 
model will allow industry to source trainees as per their requirement 
with active support from states and train them as per the requirement 
of their own organizational/industry/subsidiaries /operational need and 
provide candidates assured placement.  
Grant Thornton Bharat LLP will leverage its extensive expertise in skill development, skill gap analysis and research-
based policy advisory particularly in DDUGKY and develop a comprehensive plan and robust monitoring mechanism 
for effective implementing the Captive Employer Model under DDUGKY, focused on mobilizing, training, and 
employing rural youth in the divisions of Pune, Amravati, and Nagpur, Maharashtra.  
 
Key Objectives:  
Captive Employer is first of its kind initiative, aimed at addressing the vision of dynamic and demand-based planning 
and assist in pursuit of an efficacious skill ecosystem. The key objective of the captive model includes:

Inform the baseline with indicators for assessing maturity and readiness levels: 
A maturity assessment tool will be developed based on good practice. This will be used to inform the baseline, but 
also to measure progress over time, including benchmarking good practice. The main aim of the assessment tool is 
to determine the prospects of success of existing capability. The factors most critical to success are therefore 
explored by asking carefully designed questions, which are clustered under headings useful for decision making. 
These questions are informed and structured, based on relevant good practice theoretical constructs. 
The maturity assessment rating scale is typically based on assessing progress down a learning curve on each topic. 
A six key milestone scale on this learning curve, is shown below:  
0. Don’t know, don’t care about.  
1. Aware of need to change.  
2. + Trying something. Not yet aware of good practice. 
3. + Now know what to do and how. Aware of good practice.  
4. + Now starting to do the right things. Regular application of good practice but results still sporadic. 
5. Consistently performing well = repeated excellence, best practice institutionalised.  
Generic guidelines such as these are customised for each question, to make it easier for the interviewer to recognise 
the evidence relating to specific questions.

A. Captive Employment  
The captive employment model ensures direct engagement between industries and trainees, guaranteeing 
employment post-training and addressing the unique challenges faced by rural youth in securing jobs. It offers a 
pathway for employers to directly influence the training process, ensuring that candidates are job-ready with skills 
tailored to specific industry requirements. 
The proposed approach aims to deliver a comprehensive solution for the implementation of the captive employment 
model under the Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDUGKY). Our methodology encompasses 
the entire project lifecycle, from the identification of captive employers to ensuring sustainable employment for 
trained candidates, thereby fostering a robust skill ecosystem in the targeted divisions, particularly in the divisions 
of Pune, Amravati, and Nagpur, presents a significant opportunity to implement this model, given its diverse 
industrial base and demand for skilled workers in sectors such as manufacturing, logistics, retail, and hospitality.  
Our strategic approach is structured into the following key phases: 
 
 
This methodology ensures a seamless and integrated process for meeting the employment objectives under 
DDUGKY, thereby addressing both the workforce needs of employers and the aspirations of rural youth for gainful 
employment. 
GT will provide technical and hand holding support to strengthen the captive model under DDUGKY and support in 
identification of potential trainees as per their requirement of industry demand with active support from state to 
successfully train them as per the requirement of their own organizational/industry/subsidiaries /operational need 
and ultimately provide them assured placement.     
 
Phase 1: Identification of Captive Employers 
 
Our approach to identifying captive employers involves a detailed analysis of industry sectors with a consistent 
demand for skilled labor and a commitment to hiring trained candidates.  
 
The key activities under this phase include: 
 
• 
Sectoral Analysis: Conduct a comprehensive sectoral study to identify industries with high workforce 
demand, such as manufacturing, logistics, retail, hospitality, and healthcare. This analysis will focus on 
understanding regional labor market trends 
and identifying potential captive employment 
partners. 
• 
Employer Mapping: Develop a database of 
potential employers within each target sector 
who are actively seeking to expand their 
workforce in the Pune, Amravati, and 
Nagpur divisions. This includes targeting 
companies with large-scale operations, 
ongoing 
expansion 
plans, 
and 
a 
112
pages.113:
demonstrated interest in partnering with skill development initiatives. 
• 
Partnership Development: Engage with employers to secure their commitment to the captive employment 
model through Memorandums of Understanding (MoUs). The MoUs will specify the number of positions 
available, skill requirements, employment terms, and support for onboarding the trained candidates. 
 
By meticulously identifying captive employers, we will ensure the availability of sufficient job opportunities aligned 
with the skill sets of the trained candidates. 
 
 
Phase 2: Outreach activities 
 
Outreach activities are a critical component for establishing a network of captive employers and ensuring effective 
communication throughout the project lifecycle, includes: 
 
• 
Awareness Campaigns: Launch awareness campaigns targeting industry associations, chambers of 
commerce, and key business forums in the region to promote the captive employment model. The 
campaigns will emphasize the benefits of accessing a pre-trained workforce and highlight the incentives 
provided under DDUGKY. 
• 
Employer Engagement Events: Organize a series of employer engagement events, including CxO Meet, 
workshops, seminars, and roundtable discussions, to present the value proposition of the captive 
employment model. These events will be used to directly interact with potential employers, address their 
concerns, and showcase successful case studies from similar initiatives. 
• 
Targeted Employer Outreach: Employ a targeted outreach strategy to engage employers in high-demand 
sectors. This includes personalized meetings with HR heads and business leaders to discuss specific 
workforce needs and align the training programs with employer expectations. 
• 
Utilization of Industry Networks: Leverage existing networks, such as local industry bodies, trade unions, 
and regional business councils, to expand the outreach efforts. This will help in creating a broad employer 
base and increase the number of committed captive employment partners. 
 
Our outreach activities will foster strong relationships with industry stakeholders, ensuring their buy-in and active 
participation throughout the project. 
 
 
Phase 3: Understanding Industry Supply Scenario  
 
Understanding the industry supply scenario is essential for aligning training programs with market requirements. 
This phase will involve: 
 
• 
Skill Demand Analysis: Conduct a thorough analysis of the skill demands across various sectors, 
considering factors such as emerging job roles, technological advancements, and seasonal employment 
trends. This will involve consultations with industry experts and reviews of labor market data. 
• 
Gap Assessment: Perform a gap analysis to identify discrepancies between the current skill levels of the 
target beneficiaries and the requirements of captive employers. This analysis will inform the customization 
of training programs to bridge skill gaps effectively. 
• 
Supply Chain Mapping: Map out the supply chains of key industries in the target divisions to identify 
potential opportunities for captive employment. This involves understanding the workforce requirements at 
different points in the supply chain and developing a training plan that caters to these specific needs. 
• 
Periodic Market Assessments: Regularly update the supply scenario by conducting periodic market 
assessments and employer feedback sessions. This will help in keeping the training programs relevant and 
responsive to changing industry requirements. 
 
The insights derived from the industry supply scenario analysis will enable us to tailor the training content and 
delivery methods to meet the exact needs of captive employers. 
 
Phase 4: Onboarding and Orientation of Captive Employers 
 
To ensure a smooth integration of employers into the DDUGKY captive employment model, the following approach 
will be adopted: 
 
• 
Employer Orientation Programs: Conduct orientation sessions to familiarize employers with the DDUGKY 
guidelines, the captive employment process, and the roles and responsibilities of all stakeholders. These 
sessions will be designed to clarify the employers' expectations, obligations, and benefits under the model.

skills and training schedules based on the employer's workforce demands. This collaborative approach will 
involve employers in the curriculum design to ensure alignment with their operational needs. 
• 
Documentation and Compliance Support: Provide assistance to employers in understanding the

trainee assessments, and feedback mechanisms includes issuance of RFP to invite proposals from potential 
captive employers, conduct of thorough evaluation of proposals received, on factors such as the 
organization's track record in skill development and employment generation, capacity to provide training 
and employment opportunities, and allotment of target vide sanction order. 
• 
Dedicated Support Teams: Establish dedicated employer support teams to facilitate smooth onboarding, 
address queries, and coordinate placement activities. These teams will act as a liaison between employers, 
training partners, and candidates, ensuring seamless communication and process efficiency. 
 
The onboarding and orientation phase will establish a clear framework for collaboration with captive employers, 
thereby setting the foundation for successful placements. 
 
Phase 5: Handholding and continuous support throughout the project lifecycle. 
 
The success of the captive employment model depends on continuous support to both employers and trainee’s 
post-placement. We will coordinate, conduct, and lead the kick-off meetings with all the new project implementation 
agencies and provide end-to-end support for program-related compliance. 
The activities under this phase will include: 
 
 
• 
Post-Placement Tracking: Implement a robust post-placement tracking system to monitor the progress of 
placed candidates. This includes collecting data on job performance, satisfaction levels, and retention rates 
to identify areas for improvement. 
• 
Retention Support Programs: Offer counseling and career development programs to help candidates 
adapt to their new roles and work environments. Regular follow-up sessions will be conducted to address 
any issues faced by candidates in the workplace. 
• 
Employer Feedback Mechanism: Establish a feedback loop with employers to gather insights on the 
performance of the trained candidates and the quality of training delivered. This feedback will be used to 
refine the training programs and enhance future batches. 
• 
Ongoing Employer Engagement: Maintain regular engagement with captive employers through quarterly 
review meetings, sharing of best practices, and continuous updates on project progress. This will ensure 
the employers remain committed to the captive employment model and address any evolving workforce 
requirements. 
• 
Scaling and Replication: Based on the success and learnings of the initial phases, explore opportunities 
for scaling the captive employment model to other sectors or regions. Develop a framework for replicating 
the project in similar settings to maximize impact. 
 
Our handholding and support approach is designed to build long-term relationships with employers and ensure that 
trained youth not only secure jobs but also grow in their careers. 
 
The aforesaid approach and methodology offer a comprehensive strategy for implementing the captive employment 
model under DDUGKY in divisions namely Pune, Amravati, and Nagpur in Maharashtra. Our end-to-end enablement 
plan will create a strong foundation for sustainable employment, drive economic growth, and significantly improve 
the livelihoods of rural youth by aligning their skills with the demands of captive employers.

B. Skill Gap Assessment 
In an era marked by rapid technological advancements and shifting industry demands, the need for a well-prepared, 
skilled workforce has never been more critical. For the SMU DDUGKY, Maharashtra, conducting a comprehensive 
skill gap assessment is essential to aligning its training programs with the dynamic needs of industries. This 
assessment will enable to better understand the disparity between the skills that are currently available within the 
workforce and those that are in demand by employers, both locally and globally. 
The skill gap assessment is vital for ensuring 
that offerings remain relevant and forward-
looking. By identifying current deficiencies 
and anticipating future skill needs, SMU can 
strategically design its curricula to equip 
individuals with the competencies required in 
high-growth sectors such as advanced 
manufacturing, IT, healthcare, and renewable 
energy. Moreover, the assessment will foster 
stronger 
collaboration 
between 
SMU 
DDUGKY, 
Maharashtra 
and 
industry 
stakeholders, ensuring that training programs 
are directly informed by real-time market 
demands. 
Ultimately, 
this 
will 
enhance 
employability, reduce unemployment, and 
position as a key player in building a future-
ready workforce, capable of driving economic 
growth and innovation in both regional and 
global markets. 
 
We will conduct a comprehensive skill gap analysis to effectively align workforce capabilities with the dynamic 
demands of industries in divisions namely Pune, Amravati and Nagpur in Maharashtra. 
The scope will encompass the following: 
 
1. Sectoral Analysis 
Conduct a detailed sector-wise evaluation across key industries such as 
manufacturing, IT, healthcare, agriculture, and emerging sectors like renewable 
energy. This analysis will focus on identifying current and future skill requirements, 
job roles in demand, and existing gaps in workforce competencies. 
2. Stakeholder 
Consultations 
Engage with key stakeholders, industry leaders, employers, government agencies, 
educational institutions, and training providers to gain insights into workforce 
challenges, hiring trends, and evolving skill needs. This will be achieved through a 
combination of interviews, surveys, and focus group discussions, ensuring 
comprehensive coverage across sectors. 
3. Regional Workforce 
Profiling 
Assess the existing skill levels across different regions of state, considering 
education, vocational training, and professional qualifications. This profiling will 
focus on regional disparities in workforce readiness, highlighting areas where 
targeted interventions are required to meet industrial demands 
4. Future 
Skill 
Requirements 
 Identify emerging trends and future skill needs driven by technological 
advancements, automation, and global market shifts. This will include analysis of 
new-age skills such as digital literacy, data analytics, and advanced manufacturing 
techniques, which are critical for maintaining global competitiveness 
5. Aspiration 
Understanding aspirations of people especially those of youth in terms of sector, job 
type (wage or self- employment) etc. is critical for providing counselling and 
guidance to them. The insight will help in expectation management and linking 
people to training of their choice which in turn shall lead to higher job labour Force 
Participation & Under-employment. We will conduct aspiration mapping for the 
prospective candidates who may be enrolled under the schemes/programs. 
115
pages.116:
6. Training 
Program 
Evaluation 
and 
Recommendations 
Evaluate the existing training programs offered by SMU DDUGKY, Maharashtra and 
other institutions to identify areas of improvement and alignment with industry 
demands. Recommendations will be provided for curriculum updates, upskilling, and 
reskilling initiatives to ensure training programs are market relevant. 
 
Expected Deliverables 
Our team will conduct a comprehensive assessment to identify skill demands and placement opportunities. Skill gap 
is the interplay of industry demand, supply through public, private and non-formal/informal channels, labour force 
participation, aspiration and employability of both new entrants and existing workforce. Therefore, the proposed skill 
gap study would conduct in-depth research, analysis and inquiry into the above-mentioned areas. It would not only 
provide current and future projections but also identify bottlenecks and propose solutions. Primary research methods 
such as surveys, interviews focus group discussions observations etc. would be used for conducting skill gap study. 
The study would lead to identification of skill gap across sector, sub-sectors and job roles at district levels.              
The study in-turn will help identify the quick gain areas and areas for the long-term intervention. Special attention 
would be given to the identification of skill gap for Quick Gain & High Growth Providing sectors. 
 
1. Comprehensive Skill Gap Analysis Report:   
A detailed report providing insights into current and projected skill gaps across industries, regions, and job roles. 
This report will include quantitative and qualitative analysis based on industry feedback, workforce profiling, and 
labor market trends, offering a clear roadmap for skill development. 
2. Skill Gap Matrices:   
Industry-wise and region-specific skill gap matrices that highlight critical areas where 
skills are lacking. These matrices will serve as a reference tool for designing targeted 
training interventions and aligning workforce development efforts with industry 
requirements. 
3. Recommendations for Curriculum Enhancement:   
Tailored recommendations to update and enhance existing training programs, ensuring 
they are aligned with the identified skill gaps. This will include suggestions for new 
courses, certification programs, and upskilling initiatives that SMU can adopt to meet 
industry needs.  
4. Future Workforce Strategy:   
A strategic blueprint outlining the long-term skill development needs for key industries in 
divisions namely Pune, Amravati and Nagpur in Maharashtra, focusing on emerging 
technologies and global trends. This strategy will offer actionable steps for preparing the 
workforce for future market demands and ensuring continuous competitiveness. 
5. Presentation to Stakeholders:   
A professional presentation summarizing the key findings, insights, and recommendations from the skill gap 
assessment. This will be presented to SMU, officials and other stakeholders, providing a clear understanding of the 
workforce challenges and solutions proposed.

Sampling Process:  
The sampling process is a critical component of any research, 
particularly in large-scale assessments such as skill gap analysis. In 
the context of workforce development and industry alignment, the 
sampling process helps ensure that the collected data accurately 
represents the population being studied, thereby enabling reliable and 
actionable insights. The ideal sample size for conducting a skill gap 
assessment in divisions namely Pune, Amravati and Nagpur in 
Maharashtra depends on various factors, such as the diversity of the 
workforce, geographic distribution, key industries, and demographic 
considerations (i.e. age, education level, gender, etc.). 
The first step of the sampling process involves identifying the target 
population for the skill gap assessment includes skilled, semi-skilled 
and unskilled workers, employers and industry stakeholders and 
educational/training institutions etc.  
Given the state's economic and demographic diversity and 
considering the nature and scope of the study we propose to use two-
stage sampling method i.e. stratified random sampling method to 
sample the desired number of target respondents for the skill gap 
assessment. This method allows for the segmentation of the population into distinct subgroups (or strata) based on 
industry, geographic location, skill level, and workforce characteristics. By selecting a random sample from each 
subgroup, we ensure that the data accurately reflects the variations across different sectors and regions. 
 
Segmentation of the population: 
 
 
Determine Sample Size per Stratum/Sub-groups:  
 
The calculation of sample size involves a trade-off between statistical precision, practical constraints, and the 
objectives of the study. The determination of the sample size requires in-dept understanding of available data of 
target populations/stakeholders. Post stratification, we shall calculate the sample size for each stratum. The sample 
size for each stratum will be proportional to the population size of that stratum, or it may also be determined based 
on other considerations, such as ensuring sufficient representation from smaller region etc., decision in this regard 
will be taken post in-depth analysis and study of available data in consultation with SMU DDUGKY, Maharashtra.

The skill gap study is an extensive process that involves a series of actions engaging different sets of stakeholders. 
Qualitative data helps to capture more nuanced outcomes of the intervention including aspects such as the quality 
of the training imparted, enablers and barriers in the implementation, presence or absence of support systems, role 
of context, inclusivity of the training, among others, can be captured by incorporating the perspectives of 
stakeholders. These will form part of qualitative research. Under this component, we will undertake key informant

We also collect information available in the public domain that will help in enriching the understanding of skill gaps, 
validate findings from primary data collection efforts, and develop more informed strategies for addressing workforce 
development needs.

C. Mobilization, Alumni Support 
The methodology is divided into two key components: 
• 
Candidate Mobilization 
• 
Alumni Support 
Both components are designed to ensure that the target beneficiaries—rural youth from economically disadvantaged 
backgrounds—are not only skilled and employed but also equipped for long-term career success. 
 
Candidate Mobilization:  
Candidate mobilization is a crucial step to attract eligible rural youth to participate in the DDUGKY program. Our 
approach involves a multi-pronged strategy to reach out to potential candidates, create awareness, and encourage 
enrollment in the training programs.  
The key activities under candidate mobilization include: 
 
 
Alumni Support: 
Alumni support is vital for enhancing job retention, career growth, and the overall success of DDUGKY-trained 
candidates. Our approach focuses on providing comprehensive post-training and alumni support to help candidates 
adapt to the workplace and build sustainable careers. 
GT team will support in developing and implementing a DDU-GKY Alumni Support Strategy, including periodic 
Alumni Meets to facilitate experience sharing, networking, and feedback exchange among participants, enabling 
them to address workplace challenges, offer program improvement suggestions based on employer feedback. 
Facilitate further skilling opportunities for candidates through convergence and provide mechanisms for career 
progression.

Our team will provide a comprehensive strategy for candidate mobilization and alumni support under DDUGKY in 
the Pune, Amravati, and Nagpur divisions. By focusing on grassroots mobilization, personalized support, and long-
term engagement with alumni, we will create a sustainable model that not only provides skills and jobs but also 
empowers rural youth to thrive in their careers. 
 
D. Capacity Building, Counselling 
The proposed approach aims to enhance the capacities of key stakeholders involved in implementing the Deen 
Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDUGKY) in the Pune, Amravati, and Nagpur divisions. Capacity 
building will target State Mission Unit (SMU) officials, Project Implementing Agency (PIA) staff, and trainers, with 
additional coordination efforts involving Sector Skill Councils (SSCs) for the Training of Trainers (ToT), assessment, 
and certification. 
The methodology focuses on building a strong foundation for program management, quality assurance, training 
delivery, and compliance with DDUGKY standards.  
The key areas covered includes:  
 
 
1. Identification of Training Needs: 
The first step in the capacity-building process shall be to assess the training needs of different stakeholder 
groups. This will involve: 
121
pages.122:
Stakeholder Analysis: Conduct a comprehensive analysis to understand the roles, responsibilities, and current 
skill levels of SMU officials, PIA staff, and trainers. The analysis will identify specific training gaps in program 
management, compliance, quality assurance, and training delivery. 
Customized Training Plans: Develop customized training plans for each stakeholder group based on the 
identified gaps. This will ensure that the training content is relevant and aligned with the specific needs of each 
group, such as administrative skills for SMU officials, project management for PIA staff, and training pedagogy 
for trainers. 
 
2. Capacity Building for SMU Officials 
Capacity building for SMU officials will focus on strengthening their ability to oversee DDUGKY projects, ensure 
compliance with guidelines, and implement effective monitoring and evaluation processes. The key activities 
will include: 
• 
DDUGKY Guidelines and Compliance Training: Provide training on the latest DDUGKY guidelines, 
including administrative requirements, financial management, project reporting, and compliance 
protocols. This training will help SMU officials understand the framework for monitoring PIAs and ensure 
adherence to DDUGKY standards. 
• 
Monitoring and Evaluation Skills: Equip SMU officials with tools and techniques for effective project 
monitoring and evaluation. This will cover the use of Kaushal Bharat management information systems 
(MIS), data analysis, performance tracking, and impact assessment. 
• 
Project Guideline Updates and Best Practices Sharing: Organize periodic workshops to share 
updates on policy changes, discuss emerging trends in skill development, and share best practices from 
other states or successful projects under DDUGKY. 
 
• 
Project Oversight and Risk Management: Conduct workshops on project oversight, risk identification, 
and mitigation strategies. These workshops will enable SMU officials to address project-related risks 
proactively and ensure timely interventions when necessary. 
• 
Stakeholder Coordination: Train SMU officials on effective stakeholder engagement techniques to 
facilitate smooth coordination with PIAs, SSCs, and other government departments. This training will 
include conflict resolution, negotiation skills, and communication strategies. 
 
3. Capacity Building for PIA Staff 
The capacity-building efforts for PIA staff will focus on enhancing their project management skills, compliance 
with DDUGKY guidelines, and effective implementation of training programs. The following activities will be 
undertaken: 
• 
Project Implementation Training: Provide comprehensive training on project lifecycle management, 
including candidate mobilization, training delivery, placement facilitation, and post-placement support. 
This will help PIA staff ensure smooth project execution and achieve program targets. 
• 
Financial Management and Reporting: Train PIA staff on financial management practices, including 
budget planning, utilization of funds, documentation of expenses, and adherence to financial guidelines 
under DDUGKY. 
• 
Quality Assurance Training: Offer training on maintaining quality standards in training delivery and 
compliance with NSQF (National Skills Qualification Framework) levels. This will involve familiarizing 
PIA staff with processes for internal audits, documentation, and quality monitoring. 
• 
Mobilization Strategies: Conduct workshops on innovative mobilization techniques, outreach 
activities, and the use of digital platforms for candidate registration. The workshops will also cover 
engaging local communities and leveraging existing networks for mobilization. 
• 
Placement and Employer Engagement: Provide training on placement facilitation, employer 
partnerships, and post-placement tracking. The focus will be on understanding industry demands, 
matching candidates with suitable employers, and ensuring job retention. 
122
pages.123:
• 
Refresher Training Sessions: Organize periodic refresher training sessions for PIA staff to update 
them on changes in DDUGKY guidelines, best practices, and new training methodologies. 
• 
Peer Learning and Exchange Programs: Facilitate peer learning initiatives where PIA staff can share 
experiences, discuss challenges, and learn from successful projects. This can involve exchange visits 
to other DDUGKY projects or PIA centers with exemplary performance. 
4. Coordination with Sector Skill Councils (SSCs) for Training of Trainers (ToT), Assessment, and 
Certification: 
Coordination with SSCs is crucial to ensure that trainers are well-equipped to deliver high-quality training, 
and that assessments are conducted in line with national standards. The approach for SSC coordination 
includes: 
• 
Collaboration with SSCs: Work closely with relevant SSCs to design and deliver ToT programs that 
align with industry standards and sector-specific requirements. The ToT programs will focus on training 
pedagogy, content delivery, skill assessment, and incorporating industry-specific competencies. 
• 
Blended Learning Approach: Implement a blended learning approach combining classroom-based 
training with online modules, practical demonstrations, and hands-on activities. This will cater to trainers 
with different levels of experience and ensure a holistic learning experience. 
• 
Trainer Certification: Facilitate the certification of trainers through SSCs to ensure they meet the 
standards set by the National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC). Certification will validate trainers’ 
competencies and contribute to maintaining high training quality across DDUGKY centers. 
• 
Assessment Guidelines and Tools: Coordinate with SSCs to standardize assessment tools and 
methodologies for evaluating trainees' competencies. This will include designing sector-specific 
assessment criteria and aligning them with NSQF standards. 
• 
Third-Party Assessment: Involve SSC-certified third-party assessors to conduct evaluations of 
trainees at the end of the training programs. This independent assessment will ensure objectivity and 
adherence to national standards. 
• 
Continuous Improvement Based on Feedback: Use feedback from SSCs, trainers, and assessors to 
continuously refine assessment criteria and training methodologies. The goal is to ensure that training 
remains relevant to industry needs and produces job-ready candidates. 
5. Monitoring and Evaluation of Capacity Building Activities 
To ensure the effectiveness of capacity-building activities, a robust monitoring and evaluation (M&E) framework 
will be implemented. Key components of the M&E approach include: 
• 
Baseline Assessment: Conduct baseline assessments to measure the initial skill levels and knowledge 
of stakeholders. This will help in tailoring the training content to address the identified gaps. 
• 
Post-Training Evaluation: Perform evaluations after training programs to assess improvements in 
knowledge, skills, and competencies. This will involve surveys, feedback forms, and assessments to 
gauge the impact of the capacity-building activities. 
• 
Performance Metrics: Track KPIs such as the number of trained stakeholders, quality of training 
delivery, compliance rates, and project outcomes. This will provide insights into the effectiveness of 
capacity-building efforts and identify areas for improvement. 
• 
Periodic Reviews and Reporting: Organize periodic reviews with stakeholders to discuss progress, 
challenges, and recommendations for enhancing capacity-building activities. Regular reports will be 
prepared to document achievements and share lessons learned. 
• 
Stakeholder Feedback Mechanism: Implement a feedback mechanism to collect input from 
participants on the training content, delivery methods, and overall experience. This feedback will be 
used to make necessary adjustments and improve future capacity-building programs. 
• 
Best Practices Documentation: Document best practices and success stories from capacity-building 
initiatives. These can serve as case studies and learning resources for other stakeholders involved in 
DDUGKY implementation. 
The comprehensive capacity-building plan will not only strengthen the implementation framework but also 
contribute to better training outcomes, higher placement rates, and sustainable employment opportunities for 
DDUGKY beneficiaries.

E. Job Fairs 
The GT team will support SMU in organizing Mega Job Fairs and Industry and training partner workshops for 
improving the job availability and employment options for the youth of Maharashtra. The key activity includes but 
not limited to followings: 
 
 
F. Strategic Support Services 
Our strategic support services aim to enhance the effectiveness of the Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya 
Yojana (DDUGKY) implementation across the Pune, Amravati, and Nagpur division in Maharashtra. The 
methodology involves leveraging technology and standardized tools to ensure accurate reporting, continuous 
performance monitoring, and strategic planning. The support services will include the use of Management 
Information Systems (MIS) for reporting, tools and templates for standardized reporting, PIA (Project Implementing 
Agency) performance evaluation through scorecards, achieving training targets, project closure, and the formulation 
of annual action plans. 
The proposed approach focuses on building a robust framework that promotes transparency, accountability, and 
data-driven decision-making: 
 
Our strategic support will help in streamlining project implementation, improving data-driven decision-making, and 
achieving program objectives. By using MIS, standardized reporting tools, performance evaluation scorecards, and 
strategic planning, the proposed approach will ensure that DDUGKY projects are executed efficiently with high level 
of accountability and transparency.

Main features: Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY) is a placement linked skill 
development program for rural poor youth under National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM). It is a market-
led, placement linked skill development program for providing wage employment to rural poor youth. 
DDU-GKY follows a 3-tier implementation model. DDU-GKY is now, a State led scheme being implemented

functions as the policy-making, technical support and facilitation agency. The DDU-GKY Skills State 
Missions provide implementation support; and the Project Implementing Agencies (PIAs) implement the 
program in a PPP mode.

The PMT shall be responsible for development of a comprehensive framework for supporting,
implementing, and monitoring the Skills Acquisition and Knowledge Awareness for Livelihood
Promotion (SANKALP) programme and enabling effeclive implementation at every district level.
PMT's programme management activities will include the following tasks.
1. Performing the role of a knowledge partner of KWSDC for effective policy making and
planning

b. Benchmarking the best practices of various slale missions
c. ldentification of various interventions required for improving the employment in
the state
2. Assistance in planning, reporting, and preparation of District Skill Development Plan (DSDP)
in coordination with all stakeholders
a Preparation of State Skill Development Plan (SSDP) through aggregation of
DSDPs and analysis of district-level Monitoring & Evaluation (M&E) reports
b Preparation of PilouProject Evaluation Report for subsequent submission to
MSDE, reporting placement and coverage outcomes.
c Coordination with DSDO and concerned line departments/stakeholders for
collating the progress (financial & physical) for preparation of Annual
Performance Report in prescribed M&E Format
3. Support in management of assessment and certification process, and course curriculum
compliances
a. lntegration of all existing training programmes in the state with NSQF, NQAF,
and common norms
b. Coordination with NSDA/SSC for course accreditation, training of trainers and
candidate certification
c. Recommendation on new NSQF-aligned course development through
identification of emerging sectors4ob roles in consideration of skill gap and
impact assessment and industry consultations
d, Coordination with Sector Skill Councils/National Skill Qualification
Committee/NCVET for development and accreditation of new job roles
4. Formulation of social inclusion pilots under SANKALP
a. Recommendation of special inclusion pilots for affirmative targeting to promote
inclusion of socially excluded groups such as women,
